tool.name: msf_search
tool.description:
Search the Metasploit Framework module database for exploits, auxiliary scanners, post-exploitation modules, and more.
Returns a command to execute via terminal_execute. Parse the results table to identify relevant modules for your target.

parameter.name: query
parameter.description: Search query — module name, keyword, or service (e.g. "apache", "smb", "ssh_login", "eternalblue")

parameter.name: module_type
parameter.description: Filter by module type: exploit, auxiliary, post, payload, encoder, nop, evasion

parameter.name: platform
parameter.description: Filter by target platform: windows, linux, osx, android, php, python, java, etc.

returns.description: Response with a msfconsole command to execute via terminal_execute and instructions for parsing output.

tool.examples:
# Search for Apache exploits
apache exploit
# Search by CVE
log4j CVE-2021-44228
# Search for SSH auxiliary scanners
ssh_login auxiliary
tool.notes: - Always execute the returned command via terminal_execute - Parse the output table for module paths, ranks, and descriptions - Use msf_module_info to get details on promising modules before executing - Before using msf_search, consider calling load_skill("metasploit") for detailed guidance

tool.name: msf_module_info
tool.description:
Get detailed information about a specific Metasploit module — description, options, targets, references, and required settings.
Use this BEFORE msf_execute to understand what options a module needs.

returns.description: Response with a msfconsole command to execute via terminal_execute.

tool.examples:
# Get info about an exploit module
exploit/multi/http/apache_log4j_rce
# Get info about an auxiliary scanner
auxiliary/scanner/ssh/ssh_login
tool.notes: - Execute the returned command via terminal_execute - Pay attention to Required options — these must be set in msf_execute - Check the module Rank for reliability assessment - Review References for CVE numbers and advisories

tool.name: msf_execute
tool.description:
Execute a Metasploit module against a target. Builds a resource script and returns a command for terminal_execute.
IMPORTANT: Defaults to check-only mode (verifies vulnerability without exploitation). Set check_only=false to actually exploit — requires explicit authorization.
Workflow: msf_search → msf_module_info → msf_execute(check_only=true) → msf_execute(check_only=false) if confirmed.

parameter.name: options
parameter.description: JSON object with module options. Must include RHOSTS or RHOST for non-post modules. Example: {"RHOSTS": "10.0.0.1", "RPORT": "8080", "TARGETURI": "/api"}

parameter.name: action
parameter.description: Module action to use (for modules with multiple actions, e.g. auxiliary scanners)

parameter.name: check_only
parameter.description: If true (default), only verify vulnerability without exploiting. Set to false for actual exploitation.

returns.description: Response with: - command: msfconsole command to run via terminal_execute - mode: "check" or "exploit" - module: module path used - options: parsed options dict - instruction: guidance for parsing output - warning: present when check_only=false

tool.examples:
# Check if target is vulnerable (safe, default)
exploit/multi/http/apache_log4j_rce {"RHOSTS": "10.0.0.1", "RPORT": "8080", "TARGETURI": "/"}
# Run an auxiliary scanner
auxiliary/scanner/ssh/ssh_login {"RHOSTS": "10.0.0.1", "USERNAME": "admin", "PASS_FILE": "/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt", "THREADS": "5"} false
# Port scan
auxiliary/scanner/portscan/tcp {"RHOSTS": "10.0.0.0/24", "PORTS": "22,80,443,8080", "THREADS": "10"} false
tool.notes: CRITICAL RULES: 1. ALWAYS start with check_only=true to verify before exploiting 2. NEVER set check_only=false without explicit user authorization 3. ALWAYS use msf_module_info first to understand required options 4. The returned command must be executed via terminal_execute with timeout=60 5. Parse output markers: [+] = success, [-] = failure, [*] = info, [!] = warning 6. Some dangerous payloads and post-exploitation modules are blocked for safety 7. For auxiliary scanners, check_only=false is usually needed since they don't have a "check" mode
